dashboard.pageTagHint: Short label shown in the page switcher.

setup.subtitle: Create your admin account to finish setup.
setup.error.short: Password must be at least 8 characters.

settings.title: Settings
settings.defaults.title: Defaults
settings.defaults.subtitle: Used as the starting currency and timezone across modules.
settings.defaults.languageHint: Applies to the whole app immediately.
settings.account.title: Account
settings.account.revokeNote: Changing your password signs you out of all sessions.
settings.account.err.currentRequired: Enter your current password to confirm.
settings.network.title: Network
settings.network.subtitle: Controls who can reach this app. Changes take effect after a restart (command shown below).
settings.network.mode.localHint: Reachable only from this machine. Safest — nothing on the network can connect.
settings.network.mode.lanHint: Reachable by other devices on your network via this machine’s IP. Plain HTTP.
settings.network.mode.lanhttpsHint: Reachable on your network over HTTPS with a real certificate — no browser warnings on any device. Nothing is exposed to the internet.
settings.network.lanHttpsHelp: Uses a free Let's Encrypt certificate obtained through your DNS provider (DNS challenge), so it works on a private network without opening any port. DuckDNS is free: sign in at duckdns.org, create a subdomain, and paste its token below.
settings.network.dnsTokenHint: DuckDNS: the token shown on your duckdns.org account page. Cloudflare: an API token with DNS edit permission for this zone.
settings.network.cfDomainHint: In your Cloudflare zone, create an A record for this name pointing at this machine's LAN IP.
settings.network.lanIpHint: The DuckDNS record is pointed at this private IP automatically. Correct the prefill if it doesn't match this machine.
settings.network.ctNote: Note: issued certificates are public (Certificate Transparency), so the chosen domain name is publicly visible. The app itself stays LAN-only.
settings.network.err.dnsTokenRequired: A DNS provider token is required.
settings.network.err.lanIpRequired: This machine's LAN IP is required.
settings.network.mode.webHint: Exposed on a domain with automatic HTTPS. Only enable if this host is internet-facing.
settings.network.domainHint: Point this domain’s DNS at this host; Caddy fetches a TLS cert automatically. Served on 443.
settings.network.err.domainRequired: A domain is required for this mode.
settings.network.restartBody: Settings saved. Apply them by running this on the host:
settings.network.restartNote: The app briefly goes offline while containers recreate.
settings.modules.title: Modules
settings.modules.subtitle: Install a module to make it available in the switcher and on the dashboard. Detaching hides it and makes it inaccessible; you may also delete its stored data. Nothing is downloaded — every module ships with the app.
settings.modules.detachConfirmBody: {name} will be hidden from the switcher and dashboard and become inaccessible. You can reinstall it any time.
settings.data.title: Manage data
settings.data.subtitle: Size includes tables, indexes and large-value storage. Wiping a module permanently deletes its data and cannot be undone.
settings.data.wipeConfirmBody: This permanently deletes all {name} data ({rows} rows). Type {name} to confirm.
settings.backup.title: Backup database
settings.backup.intro: Run this on the host where the stack is deployed. It dumps the whole database over the existing Postgres connection — no extra access required.
settings.backup.encryptBody: A dump contains your data in the clear. Pipe it through gpg (or age) so the file is encrypted on disk; you'll be prompted for a passphrase.
settings.backup.security1: API keys/credentials (news feeds, data providers) are already encrypted at rest with OTW_SECRET_KEY, so they appear only as ciphertext in the dump. The backup is useless for those without that key.
settings.backup.security2: Back up OTW_SECRET_KEY (from your .env) separately — not inside the same dump — or encrypted secrets can't be restored.
settings.backup.security3: The dump includes live sessions tokens. Treat the file as a secret, or drop that table after restoring and sign in again.
settings.backup.security4: Store the encrypted backup off-box and rotate older copies.
settings.update.title: Update app
settings.update.intro: Updating rebuilds the containers, which the app can't do to itself by design — it runs without shell or Docker access to keep the attack surface small. Run these on the host:
settings.update.before1: Take a database backup first (see the Backup section).
settings.update.before2: Migrations run automatically on the new container's first boot.
settings.update.before3: Review release notes for any breaking changes.
settings.update.checkFailed: Couldn't check for updates (no connection to GitHub).
settings.logs.title: Logs
settings.logs.subtitle: Sets the minimum severity written to storage (takes effect immediately). Lower levels capture more detail and use more space.
settings.rate.title: API rate tracking
settings.rate.subtitle: Outbound calls to external data providers (market data, FX, quotes, feeds), counted since the start of the UTC day. Nothing here throttles or blocks requests — it only tracks volume and flags providers that answered with a rate-limit response.
settings.rate.noData: No outbound API calls recorded in this window yet.
settings.mcp.title: MCP
settings.mcp.subtitle: Lets AI agents (any MCP-compatible client) use this app through a controlled gateway. Each token decides which modules an agent can read or change; everything else stays out of reach. Account, network, secrets and data-wipe operations are never exposed.
settings.mcp.offNote: The MCP endpoint is off. You can prepare tokens below, but every agent request is rejected until you enable it.
settings.mcp.tokensHint: One token per agent or use case. The token is stored hashed and shown only once; revoke it any time.
settings.mcp.name: Name
settings.mcp.createdBody: Copy the token now — it is shown only once and stored hashed.
settings.mcp.connectHint: The endpoint speaks MCP over Streamable HTTP, so any compliant client works. Authenticate with a Bearer token created above; replace <TOKEN> in the snippet.
settings.mcp.note1: Agents discover what they may call via the otw_catalog tool — only the modules you granted appear.
settings.mcp.note2: Writes require the module’s read+write level; reads require at least read.
settings.mcp.note3: If the app is reachable only on localhost, agents must run on this machine too.
settings.credits.title: Credits
settings.credits.subtitle: Data sources and upstream projects each module can use — including providers available in the app that you haven't configured. Thanks to their authors.
settings.about.title: About
settings.about.subtitle: OpenTraderWorld is free, open source and self-hosted. Useful links:
settings.about.shareBody: Sharing it with a friend or your community is the best way to support the project.
settings.about.shareText: I'm using OpenTraderWorld, a free self-hosted platform for traders and investors. Check it out:
backtest.signalGroup.toggleLogicTitle: Click to switch — ALL rules must hold (AND) vs any one is enough (OR)
backtest.side.noExitRulesHint: No exit rules — exits via SL/TP or reversal.
backtest.side.exitOnReverseHint: Exit when entry conditions no longer hold
backtest.settings.reverseSideHint: Reverse side — short = inverse of long (skip configuring short)
backtest.settings.stopAndReverseHint: Stop & reverse — flip position when the opposite signal fires
backtest.settings.shortDerivedNote: Short side derived as the inverse of long.
backtest.settings.pyramidingTitle: Max stacked entries per position (1 = off). Re-fires of the entry signal add size.
backtest.settings.pyramidingNote: Up to {count} stacked entries; SL/TP track the average entry price.
backtest.page.datasetDeletedErr: Source dataset was deleted — pick another to rerun.

backtest.page.tagline: Combine indicator signals, size with pyramiding, measure the edge.
backtest.page.emptyHint: Pick a dataset, stack entry & exit conditions, then run.
backtest.page.noSavedRuns: No saved runs yet — run a strategy and save it to compare later.
